libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep the existing test JVM module opening for OGNL reflective internals: `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`. For 3.3.5, also consider adding `--add-opens=java.base/java.util=ALL-UNNAMED`, matching OGNL's own JDK 17+ test profile, especially if generated tests exercise collection/map/list accessors or reflective internals. Avoid relying on a live SecurityManager; if testing OGNL sandbox behavior, set and restore `ognl.security.manager` only within the test that needs it.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7430 [Automation] java run fails for ognl:ognl:3.3.5; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: JDK 25 permanently disables or further restricts SecurityManager behavior, so tests that require successful `System.setSecurityManager` may be brittle.
OGNL's expression compiler uses Javassist; it is transitive from `ognl:ognl:3.3.5`, so adding a duplicate explicit dependency should not be necessary unless Gradle resolution proves otherwise.
The authoritative decision on exact JVM args remains local Gradle/JDK 25 javaTest verification.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OGNL is an in-process expression/reflection library with no service or Docker requirement, and its only runtime dependency, Javassist, is already declared transitively. Meaningful tests on modern JDKs should preserve OGNL's required module-opening JVM setup.
